other_observers: Kristine Beatty.  (We were not first observers of this species, we were following up on reports by others in eBird and WhatsApp, but no one else had as of yet submitted a record.)
description: One red-orange tanager and one dull yellow tanager, both obsessively defending territory from their reflections in a car's rearview mirror and front windshield.  The yellow one would come in first from about 1/2 to 2/3 up a nearby conifer down to the vehicle and go straight for the mirror.  The red-orange one spent more time in the trees and would come in to the vehicle less often.  I am not an expert, but I believe the birds were a potential breeding pair, an adult male and an adult female.
photographer: Photographer: Diana Beatty, Diana Beatty, Diana Beatty, Diana Beatty
